findings.summary: USB_D- incorrectly reported as unprotected despite TPD2EUSB30 protecting both D+ and D-; SDIO bus (4-bit SD card), I2C, ESD protection, and PMOS power switch all detected correctly; PMOS Q1 transis...

correct.description: SDIO 4-bit bus detected with all 6 signals (CLK, CMD, D0-D3) on correctly named nets. I2C SCL/SDA found on CM4 module pins I2C_SCL0/I2C_SDA0 with no external pull-ups (accurate — template board). TPD2EUSB30 correctly identified as esd_ic protecting USB_D+/D-. PMOS Q1 (DMP2066LSN) detected with correct gate_net=SD_PWR_ON, drain_net=+3V3, source_net=SD_PWR. Schematic-to-PCB count matches at 45.

incorrect.description: The design_observations USB_D- entry has has_esd_protection=false, but U6 (TPD2EUSB30) is present on the USB_D- net (pin 2). The bug is in signal_detectors.py line 3499: 'protected_nets = {p["protected_net"] for p in results.get("protection_devices", [])}' uses the singular protected_net field (which is only 'USB_D+'), not the plural protected_nets list (['USB_D+', 'USB_D-', '__unnamed_90']). The fix is to collect all protected_nets entries. Affects any multi-rail ESD protection device.

The transistor_circuit entry for Q1 (DMP2066LSN) includes a led_driver sub-object referencing D3 ('Activity LED') with power_rail='+3V3'. However D3 is on net PI_LED_nACT (driven directly by the CM4 module, not by Q1). The actual load on Q1's source net (SD_PWR) includes D4 ('SD ON' LED) and J2 (SD card connector). The load_type='led' is triggered by D4 on SD_PWR, but the led_driver association logic looks more broadly and picks up the unrelated D3. The primary load is the SD card (J2 connector), not an LED.

The Raspberry Pi CM4 module U5 (Compute_Module_4_Functional) is split into 6 KiCad units. Each unit generates a separate subcircuit entry, resulting in 6 identical U5 entries in the subcircuits list. The bus_analysis correctly deduplicates via the KH-148 fix, but the subcircuits generation lacks the same deduplication. The correct output should have 1 U5 subcircuit entry (with the combined neighbor context). This affects any multi-unit part in a schematic.

findings.summary: 4-layer HDI PCB correctly analyzed: advanced DFM tier, 0.1016mm track width violation flagged

correct.description: The PCB analyzer correctly identifies a 4-layer board (F.Cu, B.Cu, In1.Cu, In2.Cu), 45 footprints (36 front SMD, 9 back SMD, 1 THT), 1560 track segments, 107 vias, routing complete (0 unrouted). The 0.1016mm minimum track width is correctly classified as requiring 'advanced' process (standard minimum 0.127mm). DFM tier='advanced' is accurate.

findings.summary: Template PCB correctly detected as incomplete (2 unrouted nets)

correct.description: The cm4-carrier-template PCB has routing_complete=false with 2 unrouted nets: 'Net-(C3-Pad1)' (C3.1, J3.5, J3.6) and 'Net-(J1-PadS1)' (4 pads of J1 shield). These correspond to incomplete traces in a template board. Board is 90.006x90.006mm (square, larger than the net version at 90x40mm), 4-layer. The unrouted net detection and routing_complete=false are accurate.

findings.summary: Gerber alignment flagged as misaligned due to overly tight threshold for normal copper keepout; 4-layer gerber set is complete with all required and recommended layers

correct.description: All 11 layer files present: F.Cu, B.Cu, In1.Cu, In2.Cu, F.Mask, B.Mask, F.Paste, B.Paste, F.SilkS, B.SilkS, Edge.Cuts. No missing_required or missing_recommended layers. PTH drill file present; NPTH drill absent (acceptable for this design which has no NPTH holes). Layer completeness detection is accurate.

incorrect.description: The alignment check reports aligned=false with 'Height varies by 2.1mm across copper/edge layers'. The flagged difference is B.Cu height (37.925mm) vs Edge.Cuts height (40.006mm) — a 2.08mm difference. The threshold is max(2.0, 40.006*0.05)=2.0003mm, so 2.08mm just barely exceeds it. However, a 2.08mm copper keepout from the board edge is normal engineering practice (standard IPC-A-600 recommends 0.5-2mm keepout). The 5% relative threshold produces a near-zero tolerance (2.0003mm) for a 40mm-tall board, generating false positives. Inner copper layers (In1.Cu, In2.Cu) at 39.499mm are closer to the edge as expected for plane layers. This is a borderline false positive.
